text: Attention is all you need. Transformer architecture uses self-attention to achieve state-of-the-art in machine translation.
AlphaFold predicts protein structures at atomic accuracy, revolutionizing computational biology.
Observation of gravitational waves from binary black hole merger GW150914.
CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing in human embryos shows high off-target rates. We sequenced 10 embryos and found 15% unintended mutations.
Quantum entanglement experiments demonstrate Bell inequality violations in photons at 500m separation.
High-temperature superconductivity observed in hydrogen sulfide under 200 GPa pressure at 203 K.
The Large Hadron Collider confirms the existence of the Higgs boson with mass ~125 GeV/c^2.
A method for RNA sequencing enables single-cell resolution transcriptomics across tissues.
Observation of neutrino oscillations confirms that neutrinos have mass.
Deep generative models like VAEs and GANs can synthesize realistic images and audio.
Single-molecule CRISPR imaging allows live-cell tracking of DNA editing in real time.
Observational cosmology shows accelerated expansion of the universe, consistent with dark energy models.
Topological insulators exhibit conducting edge states protected by time-reversal symmetry.
Reinforcement learning agents can learn to play Atari games from raw pixel input without supervision.
RNA-seq analysis shows differential expression patterns between healthy and diseased tissues.
Variational quantum algorithms are applied to simulate molecular energies on near-term quantum computers.
Graph neural networks model relational data in chemistry, physics, and social networks effectively.
Single-cell transcriptomics allows mapping of cell types in the developing human brain.
Transformers achieve state-of-the-art results in protein folding prediction tasks.
Topological quantum computing uses non-Abelian anyons for fault-tolerant qubits.
Diffusion models enable high-quality generative image synthesis surpassing GANs in diversity and realism.
CRISPR base editors allow precise single-nucleotide editing without double-strand breaks.
Deep reinforcement learning achieves superhuman performance in complex games using reward maximization.
Quantum teleportation transfers quantum states between distant particles using entanglement.
Lattice QCD calculations provide non-perturbative insights into strong nuclear interactions.
Black hole information paradox challenges the reconciliation of quantum mechanics and general relativity.
Neural ordinary differential equations generalize residual networks to continuous-depth models.
The Standard Model describes electromagnetic, weak, and strong interactions in particle physics.
Photonic crystals control the propagation of light using periodic dielectric structures.
Quantum error correction protects quantum information from decoherence and operational errors.
Neural Turing Machines combine neural networks with external memory for algorithmic tasks.
Wave-particle duality: quantum entities exhibit both wave-like and particle-like behavior.
Quantum key distribution enables secure communication based on quantum mechanics principles.
Spiking neural networks model neuron firing times and capture temporal information.
The AdS/CFT correspondence relates gravity in anti-de Sitter space to a conformal field theory on the boundary.
AlphaZero learns board games from self-play without human data, achieving superhuman performance.
Topological quantum computing uses anyons and braiding to perform fault-tolerant quantum operations.
Differentiable programming generalizes neural networks by allowing gradients through arbitrary code.
Quantum supremacy demonstrates computational tasks infeasible for classical computers.
Self-supervised learning pretrains models using unlabeled data and downstream fine-tuning.
Contrastive learning trains representations by distinguishing similar and dissimilar pairs of data.
Variational autoencoders model latent variables with a probabilistic encoder-decoder architecture.
The Navier-Stokes equations describe the motion of viscous fluid substances.
Phase transitions occur when a system changes from one state of matter to another, e.g., solid to liquid.
Long short-term memory (LSTM) networks solve vanishing gradient problems in RNNs.
Schrödinger equation predicts how the quantum state of a physical system evolves over time.
Boltzmann machines learn probability distributions over binary-valued data using energy-based modeling.
The backpropagation algorithm efficiently computes gradients for multilayer neural networks.
Stochastic gradient descent optimizes model parameters by computing gradients on mini-batches of data.
Capsule networks aim to preserve hierarchical pose relationships between features.
Elastic weight consolidation reduces catastrophic forgetting in continual learning.
Deep Q-Networks combine deep learning with Q-learning for end-to-end reinforcement learning.
Hopfield networks store associative memories and retrieve them via energy minimization.
Bayesian neural networks represent uncertainty by placing distributions over weights.
The Ising model explains phase transitions in ferromagnetic materials using spin interactions.
Neural style transfer modifies images by recombining content and style representations in deep networks.
Convolutional neural networks apply spatial filters to exploit local correlations in data.
Reversible neural networks allow exact reconstruction of inputs from outputs, saving memory in training.
Hyperbolic neural networks use hyperbolic geometry to embed hierarchical data efficiently.
Quantum annealing leverages quantum tunneling to find low-energy states of combinatorial optimization problems.
Generative adversarial networks (GANs) train a generator and discriminator in a minimax game to synthesize realistic data.
Meta-learning enables models to adapt quickly to new tasks using prior experience across tasks.
Layer normalization stabilizes training in deep neural networks by normalizing activations across features.
Neural architecture search (NAS) automates the design of optimal neural network architectures.

def binary_search(arr, target):
    low, high = 0, len(arr)-1
    while low <= high:
        mid = (low+high)//2
        if arr[mid] == target: return mid
        elif arr[mid] < target: low = mid+1
        else: high = mid-1
    return -1

def reverse_linked_list(head):
    prev = None
    while head:
        next_node = head.next
        head.next = prev
        prev = head
        head = next_node
    return prev

def has_cycle(head):
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
        if slow == fast: return True
    return False

theorem sqrt2_irrational: "sqrt 2 ∉ ℚ" proof assume "sqrt 2 ∈ ℚ" ... qed
∀ ε > 0, ∃ N ∈ ℕ such that ∀ n ≥ N, |a_n - L| < ε. This is the definition of a convergent sequence.
Euler's formula: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0.
The Riemann Hypothesis states that all non-trivial zeros of the zeta function have real part 1/2.
Prove by induction: sum_{i=1}^n i = n(n+1)/2.
Definition of a group: (G, *) is a group if closure, associativity, identity, and inverse properties hold.
Proof that there are infinitely many primes: assume finite, construct product +1, leads to contradiction.
Definition: A metric space (X, d) satisfies positivity, symmetry, triangle inequality, and d(x, y) = 0 iff x = y.
Cantor's diagonalization proof shows that the real numbers are uncountable.
Fundamental theorem of calculus: If f is continuous on [a, b], then ∫_a^b f'(x) dx = f(b) - f(a).
The Pythagorean theorem: In a right triangle, a^2 + b^2 = c^2.
Definition: A topology on a set X is a collection of subsets T ⊆ P(X) closed under union and finite intersection.
Proof that e is irrational: assume e = p/q, expand as series, leads to contradiction.
Lagrange multipliers provide necessary conditions for constrained local extrema of functions.
Definition: A field is a set F with two operations (+, *) satisfying closure, associativity, distributivity, identity, and inverses.
Taylor series: f(x) = Σ (f^n(a)/n!)*(x-a)^n converges to f(x) under certain conditions.
The axiom of choice: For any set of non-empty sets, there exists a choice function selecting one element from each set.
Fermat's Little Theorem: If p is prime and a ∈ ℤ, then a^p ≡ a (mod p).
Definition: A vector space V over a field F has vector addition and scalar multiplication satisfying 8 axioms.
Proof: The sum of the angles of a triangle in Euclidean space equals 180°.
Definition: An inner product space is a vector space with a positive-definite bilinear form ⟨·,·⟩.
Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: |⟨x,y⟩| ≤ ||x||·||y|| for all vectors x, y in inner product space.
Definition: A normed space is a vector space with a function ||·|| satisfying positivity, scalar multiplication, and triangle inequality.
Proof: The derivative of sin(x) is cos(x) using limit definition of derivative.
Definition: A sequence {a_n} is Cauchy if ∀ ε>0 ∃ N s.t. m,n ≥ N ⇒ |a_m - a_n|<ε.
Fundamental theorem of algebra: Every non-constant polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root.
Definition: A linear transformation T: V→W satisfies T(v + u) = T(v) + T(u) and T(c·v) = c·T(v).
Binomial theorem: (x + y)^n = Σ_{k=0}^n (n choose k) x^{n-k} y^k.
Definition: A topology is Hausdorff if any two distinct points have disjoint neighborhoods.
Definition: A differentiable manifold is a topological manifold with a smooth structure.
Green's theorem: ∮_C (L dx + M dy) = ∬_D (∂M/∂x - ∂L/∂y) dA.
Stokes' theorem: ∮_∂S F·dr = ∬_S (∇×F)·dS.
Fermat's Last Theorem: No three positive integers a, b, c satisfy a^n + b^n = c^n for n > 2.
The Banach–Tarski paradox shows a solid sphere can be decomposed and reassembled into two identical spheres.
The Fourier Transform converts a function from its time domain to frequency domain: F(ω) = ∫ f(t)e^(-iωt) dt.
The Poincaré Conjecture: Every simply connected, closed 3-manifold is homeomorphic to the 3-sphere.
Lagrange's four-square theorem: every natural number is the sum of four integer squares.
Gödel's incompleteness theorem: any consistent system capable of arithmetic has true statements that are unprovable.
A proof by induction example: sum_{i=1}^n i^2 = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6.
Taylor series expansion of e^x: e^x = Σ (x^n)/n! for n=0 to ∞.
Maclaurin series for sin(x): sin(x) = Σ (-1)^n x^(2n+1)/(2n+1)! for n=0 to ∞.
The Gamma function generalizes the factorial: Γ(n) = ∫_0^∞ x^(n-1)e^(-x) dx.
The zeta function ζ(s) = Σ n^-s encodes the distribution of prime numbers in its zeros.
The Prime Number Theorem: π(n) ~ n / ln(n), describing the asymptotic distribution of primes.
The Lagrange interpolation formula reconstructs a polynomial through n+1 points.
The Legendre polynomials P_n(x) solve Legendre's differential equation.
The Hermite polynomials H_n(x) appear in solutions to the quantum harmonic oscillator.
The Chebyshev polynomials T_n(x) minimize the maximum error in polynomial approximation.
The Bessel function J_n(x) solves Bessel's differential equation: x^2 y'' + x y' + (x^2-n^2)y=0.
The Laplace transform: F(s) = ∫_0^∞ e^(-st) f(t) dt, converting functions from time to complex frequency domain.
The Möbius function μ(n) is defined as 0 if n has squared prime factor, (-1)^k if n is product of k distinct primes.
Euler's totient function φ(n) counts integers up to n that are coprime to n.
The Dirichlet theorem on arithmetic progressions: every arithmetic progression a, a+d, a+2d,... with gcd(a,d)=1 contains infinitely many primes.
Stirling's approximation: n! ≈ sqrt(2πn) (n/e)^n, useful for asymptotics.
The Weierstrass function is continuous everywhere but differentiable nowhere.
The Cantor set is uncountable, totally disconnected, and has measure zero.
The Heine-Borel theorem: a subset of ℝ^n is compact iff it is closed and bounded.
The Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem: every bounded sequence in ℝ^n has a convergent subsequence.
The Rouché's theorem gives conditions for the number of zeros of complex functions within a contour.
Liouville's theorem: every bounded entire function must be constant.
The Cauchy integral formula provides the value of a holomorphic function inside a contour in terms of its values on the contour.
The residue theorem evaluates complex line integrals using residues of poles inside the contour.
Schur's lemma: in representation theory, a linear map commuting with irreducible representations is scalar.
The Jordan canonical form represents a linear operator by a block-diagonal matrix of Jordan blocks.

Spacecraft thermal protection system using ablative materials and active cooling channels.
Seismic base isolation for buildings using friction pendulum bearings.
How to change a car tire: 1. Loosen lug nuts. 2. Jack up the car. 3. Remove lug nuts and tire. 4. Mount spare tire. 5. Tighten lug nuts. 6. Lower car. 7. Final tighten.
How to boil an egg: Place egg in pot, cover with water, bring to boil, turn off heat, cover, let sit for 9-12 minutes, then ice bath.
How to assemble a bookshelf: Unpack, attach sides, screw shelves, tighten screws.
How to bake chocolate chip cookies: Cream butter and sugar, add eggs and vanilla, mix dry ingredients, combine, fold in chips, drop onto sheet, bake at 375°F for 9-11 minutes.
How to unclog a sink: Remove standing water, use plunger, try baking soda and vinegar, if needed remove trap and clean.
How to plant a tree: Dig hole twice width of root ball, place tree, backfill soil, water thoroughly, mulch.
How to change engine oil: Warm up engine, lift car, drain oil, replace filter, refill with new oil, check level.
How to do a push-up: Place hands shoulder-width apart, keep body straight, lower chest to ground, push back up.
How to write a Python loop: for i in range(10): print(i)
How to take a portrait photo: Use natural light, focus on eyes, choose a clean background, adjust aperture for blur.
How to tie a tie: Drape around neck, cross wide end over narrow, bring up through loop, down through knot, tighten.
How to sew a button: Thread needle, knot end, push through fabric and button holes several times, wrap thread under button, knot.
How to set up a smart speaker: Plug in, download app, connect to Wi-Fi, follow voice prompts.
How to build a LEGO car: Gather wheels, chassis, bricks; assemble base, add wheels, body, and details.
How to play a C chord on guitar: Place index on 1st fret B string, middle on 2nd fret D string, ring on 3rd fret A string, strum.
How to do the moonwalk: Start with feet together, slide one foot back while keeping weight on other, switch smoothly.
How to solder wires: Strip insulation, twist wires, heat joint with iron, apply solder, let cool.
How to 3D print an object: Download STL file, slice in software, load filament, start print, remove when done.
How to start a campfire: Gather tinder, kindling, fuel wood; build teepee or log cabin; light tinder.
How to perform CPR: Check responsiveness, call 911, do 30 chest compressions, then 2 rescue breaths, repeat.
How to make a smoothie: Add fruit, yogurt, liquid, and ice to blender; blend until smooth.
How to change a diaper: Place baby on safe surface, remove dirty diaper, clean area, put on new diaper, secure tabs.
How to fold a paper airplane: Fold paper in half lengthwise, fold corners to center, fold again to make wings.
How to train a dog to sit: Hold treat above nose, move back, when dog sits say 'sit' and reward.
How to play chess: Set up board, pieces move differently, goal is checkmate opponent's king.
How to knit a scarf: Cast on stitches, knit every row until desired length, cast off.
How to hang a picture: Measure and mark, hammer in nail or wall anchor, hang picture, level.
How to replace a smartphone screen: Heat edges, remove screws, disconnect cables, replace screen, reassemble.
How to declutter a room: Sort items into keep, donate, trash; put keep items in designated spots.
How to bake bread: Mix flour, water, yeast, salt; knead; let rise; shape; rise again; bake at 450°F.
How to arrange furniture: Measure room, plan focal point, leave walkways, balance visual weight.
How to create a budget: List income, track expenses, categorize, set spending limits, review monthly.
How to groom a dog: Brush fur, trim nails, clean ears, bathe with dog shampoo, dry.
How to set up a tent: Lay out footprint, assemble poles, attach to tent body, stake down, add rainfly.
How to fly a drone: Charge batteries, calibrate compass, take off slowly, use gentle stick movements.
How to sail a small boat: Understand wind direction, adjust sails, steer with tiller, tack or jibe to turn.
How to fix a bike chain: Use chain tool to push pin, remove broken link, replace with new link or reconnect.
How to tie a bowline knot: Form loop, pass end up through loop, around standing part, back down loop, tighten.
How to build a birdhouse: Cut wood pieces, assemble with screws, drill entrance hole, sand, paint or stain.
How to weld metal: Clean surfaces, set welder amperage, strike arc, move in steady motion.
How to thread a sewing machine: Place spool, guide thread through tension discs, take-up lever, needle eye.
How to write calligraphy: Hold pen at 45°, apply light pressure on upstrokes, heavy on downstrokes.
How to brew beer at home: Sanitize equipment, boil malt extract and hops, cool, add yeast, ferment 2 weeks, bottle.
How to do downward dog: Start on hands and knees, lift hips up, straighten legs, press heels toward floor.
How to throw a jab in boxing: Extend lead hand straight, rotate fist, keep other hand guarding.
How to give a speech: Outline key points, practice, make eye contact, speak clearly, use gestures.
How to edit a photo in Lightroom: Adjust exposure, contrast, white balance, crop, and export.
How to cut video in Premiere: Import clips, drag to timeline, use razor tool to cut, delete unwanted sections.
How to model a donut in Blender: Add torus, scale, apply subdivision surface, sculpt details, add materials.
How to mix a song: Set levels, pan instruments, EQ, compress, add reverb and effects, bounce to stereo.
How to improve grammar: Read regularly, practice writing, use grammar check tools, learn common rules.
How to safely handle raw chicken: Keep separate from other foods, wash hands and surfaces, cook to 165°F internal.
How to plan a trip: Choose destination, book flights and accommodation, research activities, pack light.
How to plan a party: Set date and budget, create guest list, choose venue, send invites, plan food and music.
How to replace a light switch: Turn off power, remove cover, unscrew switch, disconnect wires, connect new switch, reassemble.
How to fix a leaky faucet: Turn off water, disassemble handle, replace washer or cartridge, reassemble.
How to detail a car interior: Vacuum thoroughly, clean upholstery and carpets, wipe down all surfaces, condition leather.
How to paint a room: Prepare walls with spackle and sand, tape trim, apply primer, paint with roller in 'W' pattern.
How to start a vegetable garden: Choose sunny spot, prepare soil, plant seeds or seedlings, water regularly, weed.
How to use a fire extinguisher: Pull pin, aim at base of fire, squeeze handle, sweep side to side.
How to sharpen a knife: Hold at 20° angle against whetstone, push blade forward, repeat on other side.
How to make coffee with a French press: Add coarse grounds, pour hot water, stir, steep 4 minutes, press plunger down.
How to meditate: Sit comfortably, close eyes, focus on breath, when mind wanders gently return to breath.
How to wrap a gift: Place box on wrapping paper, cut to size, fold edges, tape securely, add ribbon.
FreeLaw provides access to US legal opinions, statutes, and case law.
MultiLegal aggregates legal texts from multiple jurisdictions for comparative studies.
MIMIC contains de-identified clinical data for research in critical care and patient outcomes.
Clinical notes datasets provide structured and unstructured patient records for research and model training.
Electronic health record (EHR) corpora contain longitudinal patient data for predictive modeling.
Patent datasets include USPTO and EPO filings for technology trend analysis and research.
Biomedical literature datasets aggregate research papers for NLP tasks and scientific discovery.
Court case summaries provide concise overviews of judicial opinions for legal research.
Medical imaging datasets provide annotated scans for training diagnostic models.
Pharmacological datasets contain information on drugs, dosages, and therapeutic indications.
Clinical trials registries provide structured data on trial design, participants, and outcomes.
Legal precedent datasets organize historical rulings for case-based reasoning and AI research.
Clinical decision support datasets include structured patient features and intervention outcomes.
Legal contract corpora provide annotated agreements for NLP-based contract analysis.
Drug-drug interaction datasets document known pharmacological interactions for model training.
Anatomical atlases provide structured reference data for organ systems, tissues, and imaging.
Regulatory datasets contain FDA and EMA compliance data for medical devices and drugs.
Disease ontologies provide hierarchical structured knowledge of diseases and their relationships.
Patent citation networks model technological dependencies and innovation flows.
Clinical practice guidelines provide sequential recommendations for diagnosis and treatment.
Legal citation graphs structure relationships between cases and precedents for retrieval tasks.
Medical ontology mappings connect diseases, drugs, and procedures across datasets for interoperability.
Clinical imaging benchmarks provide annotated datasets for evaluating medical AI models.
Biomedical knowledge graphs integrate multiple sources for research in drug discovery and genomics.
Legal briefs corpora provide structured and unstructured summaries for AI-assisted legal analysis.
Clinical trial outcome datasets provide structured results for meta-analysis and evidence synthesis.
Patent technology classifications categorize inventions for trend analysis and retrieval.
Healthcare procedure coding datasets standardize medical interventions for billing and research.
Regulatory submission datasets provide structured information on applications for drugs and devices.
Legal ontology datasets map concepts and relationships for NLP-driven case analysis.
Drug efficacy datasets provide structured clinical trial data on treatment outcomes.
Medical device datasets contain information on design, use, and clinical evaluation for AI research.
Medical coding standards datasets provide mappings between ICD, CPT, and SNOMED codes.
Legal taxonomies categorize statutes, regulations, and case law for AI-assisted search.
Clinical trial protocols provide structured experimental design information for reproducibility.
Healthcare cost datasets document billing and reimbursement data for economic modeling.
Patent full-text corpora allow extraction of claims, abstracts, and prior art citations.
Clinical outcome registries aggregate patient results to support comparative effectiveness research.
Legal opinions annotated datasets provide metadata for AI-driven reasoning and retrieval.
Medical imaging segmentation datasets include pixel-level annotations for supervised learning.
Drug-target interaction datasets link pharmaceutical compounds to biological mechanisms.
Clinical phenotype datasets map patient characteristics to disease categories for precision medicine.
Patent litigation datasets track lawsuits and oppositions for IP risk analysis.
Medical ontology expansion datasets connect new diseases, procedures, and drugs to existing knowledge graphs.
Legal case outcome prediction datasets provide structured inputs for machine learning models.
Medical guidelines NLP datasets encode procedural steps from textual protocols.
Pharmacovigilance datasets track adverse drug events for safety monitoring.
Patent metadata datasets capture filing dates, inventors, and classifications for trend analysis.
Clinical trial adverse event datasets detail reported side effects for safety research.
Legal regulatory corpora contain statutes, regulations, and administrative codes.
Hospital operational datasets include resource usage, bed occupancy, and staffing information.
Drug dosage datasets provide structured instructions for pharmacological studies.
Medical procedure coding datasets link clinical interventions to standardized codes.
Legal annotation corpora label case law with issues, outcomes, and precedents.
Medical procedure outcome datasets provide structured results for effectiveness evaluation.
Patent family datasets link related patents across jurisdictions for innovation tracking.
Clinical trial eligibility datasets include inclusion/exclusion criteria for patient selection.
Drug discovery assay datasets provide experimental results for compound screening.
Legal statute corpora organize laws by jurisdiction and topic for retrieval and AI analysis.
Medical sensor datasets provide physiological measurements for predictive modeling.
Drug chemical structure datasets provide molecular graphs for cheminformatics applications.
Hospital workflow datasets track patient movement and procedure timelines.
Clinical trial metadata datasets provide structured contextual information for reproducibility.
Legal citation annotation datasets enrich case law with references for NLP-driven retrieval.
